title: On the Architecture of the Wild
author: From: John Muir
content: The clearest way into the Universe is through a forest wilderness. When you are lost in the details of a project, step back and view the larger ecosystem of your goals.
action: Action: Step away from your desk for 15 minutes; observe a natural process (even if it's just weather or traffic) and relate its patience to your current project.

title: The Principle of Adaptation
author: From: Charles Darwin
content: It is not the strongest of the species that survives, nor the most intelligent; it is the one most adaptable to change. Your systems must be as fluid as the environment they inhabit.
action: Action: Identify one 'fixed' rule in your workflow and create a scenario where you would intentionally break it.

title: The Focusing Illusion
author: From: Daniel Kahneman
content: Nothing in life is as important as you think it is while you are thinking about it. Give your current crisis the appropriate, measured level of concern—no more, no less.
action: Action: Scale your current 'urgent' stress level down from a 9 to a 4.

title: The Law of Reciprocity
author: From: Robert Cialdini
content: Human beings are hard-wired to return favors. Strategic generosity—giving value before asking for it—is the most reliable way to build a network.
action: Action: Send one brief, helpful resource to a colleague or collaborator with no expectation of a return favor.

title: The Feedback Loop
author: From: Systems Theory
content: Negative feedback loops stabilize a system; positive feedback loops accelerate it. You need both to survive and thrive.
action: Action: Create a 'check' (negative loop) that prevents you from going off-track during your next deep-work session.
